modules.name: Introduction to Agile and Scrum
modules.description: Foundations of Agile philosophy, the Agile Manifesto, history of Scrum, comparison with traditional project management, and the Agile mindset.

lessons.title: Agile Manifesto & Principles
topics: Overview of the Agile Manifesto
The 4 values and 12 principles
Why Agile was created and what it replaced
Discussion: Applying Agile values to real projects
Interactive activity: Agile Value Sorting

lessons.title: History and Evolution of Scrum
topics: Origins of Scrum (Takeuchi & Nonaka, Jeff Sutherland, Ken Schwaber)
Timeline of Scrum development (from 1990s to present)
Key publications and industry adoption
Scrum's role in the Agile ecosystem
Visual timeline creation (group task)

lessons.title: Comparing Agile to Traditional Project Management
topics: Waterfall vs Agile: life cycle, documentation, change management
Project Triangle: Scope, Cost, Time in Agile vs Waterfall
Case Study: Compare outcomes of a Waterfall vs Scrum project
Group Discussion: Which model fits which type of project?

lessons.title: Agile Mindset & Team Culture
topics: What is the Agile mindset?
Collaboration, trust, and self-organization
Psychological safety in Scrum teams
Team exercise: Role-play scenarios that build or break Agile culture
Debrief discussion: Reflecting on your team's mindset

modules.name: Scrum Framework Overview
modules.description: The foundational structure of Scrum — pillars, values, process flow, comparison with other Agile frameworks, and building effective Agile teams.

lessons.title: Scrum Pillars and Values
topics: The 3 Pillars of Scrum: Transparency, Inspection, Adaptation
The 5 Scrum Values: Commitment, Courage, Focus, Openness, Respect
Group activity: Pillar vs Value Match
Discussion: Why values matter more than processes

lessons.title: Scrum Process Flow
topics: The Scrum lifecycle: Sprint Planning to Retrospective
Iterative vs Incremental development
Delivering value in every Sprint
Interactive diagram: Build the Scrum process map
Simulation: A Day in the Life of a Scrum Team

lessons.title: Key Differences: Scrum vs Other Agile Frameworks
topics: Compare Scrum to: Kanban, XP, SAFe, Lean Software Development
Side-by-side comparison table activity
Pros and cons of each model
Case study: Which framework fits which context?

lessons.title: Building Agile Teams
topics: Scrum Team characteristics: cross-functional, self-organizing, collaborative
Ideal team size and composition
Tuckman's Team Development Model in Scrum context
Team exercise: Design Your Agile Dream Team
Peer review: Present and critique each team's setup

modules.name: Scrum Roles and Responsibilities
modules.description: The three core Scrum roles — Product Owner, Scrum Master, and Development Team — their responsibilities, interactions, and accountability.

lessons.title: Product Owner — Vision, Backlog, Stakeholders
topics: Role of the Product Owner: product vision, backlog ownership, prioritization
Acts as bridge between stakeholders and team
Writing user stories, stakeholder engagement, backlog refinement
Activity: Write a Product Vision Statement
Scenario discussion: What happens when the PO is unavailable?

lessons.title: Scrum Master — Servant Leadership and Facilitation
topics: Scrum Master as: Coach, Facilitator, Impediment remover, Process guardian
Misconceptions: Not a manager, not a project lead
Facilitate ceremonies, shield team, foster Agile mindset
Group activity: Scrum Master in Action role-play
Discussion: What does servant leadership look like?

lessons.title: Development Team — Cross-functional Collaboration
topics: Self-organizing, cross-functional teams (3–9 members)
Shared accountability and collective ownership
Estimation, planning, delivering increments
Exercise: Build a Dream Team
Discussion: Transitioning from silos to cross-functional collaboration

lessons.title: Role Interactions and Accountability
topics: How three roles collaborate across Scrum events
Common anti-patterns: PO as project manager, SM taking on tasks, devs ignoring backlog
Activity: Analyze case studies to identify role conflict
Group discussion: What makes accountability clear or confusing?

modules.name: Scrum Ceremonies
modules.description: The five formal Scrum events — Sprint Planning, Daily Scrum, Sprint Review, Sprint Retrospective — their purpose, structure, and best practices.

lessons.title: Sprint Planning
topics: Purpose: Define Sprint Goal and deliverables
Inputs: Product Backlog, team capacity, past performance
Outputs: Sprint Goal, Sprint Backlog
Format: Part 1 (What?) and Part 2 (How?)
Activity: Simulate a Sprint Planning meeting
Discussion: What makes a Sprint Goal effective?

lessons.title: Daily Scrum
topics: Purpose: Synchronize team activity toward Sprint Goal
Format: 15-minute stand-up, same time/place daily
Common format: What did I do? What will I do? Blockers?
Anti-patterns: Status reporting, problem-solving during meeting
Roleplay: Mock Daily Scrums with good and bad facilitation
Tips for distributed/remote teams

lessons.title: Sprint Review
topics: Purpose: Inspect increment and adapt Product Backlog
Who attends: Scrum Team + Stakeholders
Structure: Demo, discuss, capture feedback
Emphasis on working product, not slide decks
Case Study: Sprint Review done right vs wrong
Group Activity: Design a Sprint Review agenda

lessons.title: Sprint Retrospective
topics: Purpose: Inspect team process and plan improvements
Who attends: Scrum Team only
Structure: What went well? What didn't? What to improve?
Techniques: Start/Stop/Continue, 4Ls, Sailboat, Mad/Sad/Glad
Simulation: Conduct a team Retrospective
Discussion: What prevents honest retrospectives?

modules.name: Scrum Artifacts
modules.description: The three Scrum artifacts — Product Backlog, Sprint Backlog, and Increment — along with the Definition of Done and artifact transparency.

lessons.title: Product Backlog
topics: Definition and purpose
Owned and prioritized by the Product Owner
Characteristics: emergent, ordered, prioritized by value
Backlog items: epics, user stories, bugs, spikes
Backlog Refinement: frequency, participants, techniques
Activity: Refine a messy product backlog
Best practices: INVEST criteria

lessons.title: Sprint Backlog
topics: Definition and ownership by Development Team
Created during Sprint Planning
Selected Product Backlog Items + plan for delivery
Living document updated daily
Task breakdown and estimation (story points, hours)
Burn-down charts
Group activity: Create a Sprint Backlog

lessons.title: Increment and Definition of Done
topics: The Increment: potentially shippable working software
What makes an Increment done?
Definition of Done (DoD): shared team agreement
DoD vs Acceptance Criteria
Examples of good and bad DoDs
Activity: Write a DoD for a web application
Discussion: How does a weak DoD affect quality?

lessons.title: Artifact Transparency and Refinement
topics: Importance of clear, visible, accessible artifacts
Tools: Jira, Trello, Azure DevOps, ClickUp
Risks of hidden or outdated artifacts
Anti-patterns: stale backlogs, hidden work, inconsistent DoD
Hands-on: Explore artifact tracking and visualization
Peer Review: Share artifact setup and get feedback

modules.name: Implementing Scrum in a Team
modules.description: Real-world Scrum adoption — organizational readiness, coaching teams, stakeholder management, and scaling Scrum across multiple teams.

lessons.title: Preparing an Organization for Scrum
topics: Organizational mindset shift: from control to collaboration
Identifying enablers and blockers in company culture
Management's role in supporting self-organizing teams
Agile Champions, Communities of Practice, and internal advocacy
Activity: Scrum Readiness Assessment
Discussion: Handling resistance to Agile practices

lessons.title: Forming and Coaching Scrum Teams
topics: Team formation: size, skill diversity, autonomy
Importance of onboarding and goal alignment
Coaching strategies for Scrum Masters
Stages of team maturity (Tuckman model revisited)
Workshop: Design a team launch plan
Discussion: Balance between self-organization and guidance

lessons.title: Managing Stakeholders and Business Expectations
topics: Stakeholder mapping and communication strategy
Balancing business priorities with team capacity
Sprint Reviews as engagement tools
Transparent reporting (velocity, burndown)
Activity: Write a stakeholder communication plan
Case Study: Agile stakeholder management successes and failures

lessons.title: Scaling Scrum with Multiple Teams
topics: When and why to scale Scrum
Scaling frameworks: Nexus, Scrum@Scale, SAFe, LeSS
Cross-team coordination and dependency management
Shared Product Backlogs and integration strategies
Activity: Create a coordination plan for 3 teams
Discussion: Is scaling your first move or last resort?

modules.name: Advanced Scrum Topics
modules.description: Metrics, technical debt management, Agile tooling, and adapting Scrum for complex or regulated environments.

lessons.title: Metrics in Scrum — Velocity, Burn-down, Burn-up
topics: Velocity: what it is and what it isn't
Burn-down vs Burn-up charts
Cumulative Flow Diagrams (CFDs)
Using metrics to inspect and adapt
Misuse of metrics: vanity vs value
Activity: Interpret sample Sprint data

lessons.title: Managing Technical Debt and Continuous Improvement
topics: What is technical debt? Causes and consequences
Incorporating refactoring into Sprints
Definition of Done and technical quality
Continuous improvement through retrospectives and Kaizen
Case Study: Ignored vs addressed technical debt
Workshop: Create a technical debt reduction plan

lessons.title: Agile Tools (Jira, Trello, Azure Boards, etc.)
topics: Overview: Jira, Trello, Azure DevOps, ClickUp
Tool comparison: strengths and use cases
Setting up Scrum Boards: columns, workflows, labels
Automations and reports
Hands-on lab: Create a simple Scrum board
Peer walkthrough: Showcase and critique tool setups

lessons.title: Adapting Scrum in Complex or Regulated Environments
topics: Challenges: compliance, documentation, integration with non-Agile teams
Strategies for regulated industries (finance, healthcare, defense)
Hybrid models: Water-Scrum-Fall
Risk management and documentation in Scrum
Scenario activity: Plan a Sprint for a regulated project
Discussion: How far can you stretch Scrum?

modules.name: Capstone Project and Certification Prep
modules.description: Course synthesis — review of core concepts, capstone sprint simulation, troubleshooting common Scrum issues, and certification readiness.

lessons.title: Review of Core Concepts and Group Retrospective
topics: Facilitated recap: roles, ceremonies, artifacts, metrics, tools
Group activity: Scrum Jeopardy or Quiz Bowl
Team retrospective: What did we learn? What would we improve?
Discussion: Which Scrum value was most relevant?

lessons.title: Final Capstone Sprint Simulation
topics: Simulation setup: form Scrum Teams with assigned roles
Given a fictional product brief
Sprint Execution: Planning, condensed Sprint, Review, Retrospective
Real-time feedback from instructors or observers

lessons.title: Troubleshooting Common Scrum Issues
topics: Common problems: Scrum-but, role confusion, poor backlog management, stakeholder disengagement
Problem-solving workshop: analyze scenarios, identify root causes
Activity: Scrum Clinic — diagnose fictional team dysfunctions

lessons.title: Final Quiz, Peer Review, and Certification Readiness
topics: Final Quiz (15–20 questions): scenario-based, aligned to certification formats
Peer review: Evaluate Capstone Sprint performance
Certification overview: PSM, CSM, PMI-ACP
Exam tips, prep strategies, and free resources
